finding: The repeated peer-terminal crash contract needs retained execution evidence across distinct operating-system environments; source-level test presence alone is insufficient.
design_implication: Run the exact committed stress test on Linux and macOS, retain runner identity and raw test logs, and keep the workflow fail-open only for artifact upload, not for test success.

verification.detail: The workflow was fetched back from the exact commit and contains both matrix runners, exact test invocation, pipefail, always-on artifact upload, and a 30-day retention request.

evidence_strength: committed executable workflow; no passing workflow run claimed
uncertainty: GitHub-hosted runner image versions may change over time.
Filesystem type metadata does not prove every durability semantic exercised by the operating system.
A passing run proves the bounded injected scenarios only, not all possible crash timings.
falsification_route: A workflow run fails on either operating system.
Residual lock, stale, or temporary artifacts remain after any cycle.
The terminal journal contains more than one terminal for the shared trigger.
The retained artifact is missing runner identity or the raw test log.
